prompt: Fix the PostgreSQL connection issue in the WorldPulse project. The scraper and API are trying to connect to databases named 'worldpulse' and 'postgres' but the actual database is 'worldpulse_db'. Find all .env files, docker-compose configs, and database connection strings in the project and ensure they all consistently use 'worldpulse_db' as the database name. Verify by running the connection test. Do not ask for confirmation — make the fix and move on.

Build a scraper health dashboard for WorldPulse. In apps/scraper/, add: (1) A health tracker that records per-source status (last_seen, error_count, success_rate, latency_ms) to Redis; (2) An API endpoint GET /api/v1/admin/scraper/health that returns the status of all sources; (3) Dead-source detection: if a source has not returned a signal in 30 minutes, mark it as 'stale' and log a warning; (4) A simple health summary log line printed every 5 minutes showing: total sources, healthy, stale, failed. Write TypeScript, strict mode, no confirmation prompts needed — just build and validate.
Add production-grade retry logic and circuit breaker pattern to the WorldPulse scraper pipeline in apps/scraper/.
Add Kafka consumer group lag monitoring to the WorldPulse infrastructure.
Wire the WorldPulse world map to live data.
Connect the WorldPulse map to the WebSocket feed so new signals appear in real time without page refresh.
Build the complete search indexing pipeline for WorldPulse.
Build the search feature end-to-end for WorldPulse.
Build the complete signal detail page for WorldPulse at /signals/[id].
Build complete user profile pages for WorldPulse at /users/[handle].

phase: Phase 5a — UI Polish: Skeletons, Empty States, Toasts
prompt: Add polish across the WorldPulse frontend.
Make WorldPulse fully responsive for mobile and tablet.

phase: Phase 6a — Verification Engine: Reliability Scoring UI
prompt: Wire the verification engine output to the WorldPulse UI.
Build a new user onboarding flow for WorldPulse.
Add a test suite and security hardening to WorldPulse.

phase: Gate 1 — Scraper Stability Validation & 14-Day Health Monitor
prompt:
Validate and harden the WorldPulse scraper pipeline so it is provably stable enough to start the 14-consecutive-day stability clock — a hard launch prerequisite.

Do ALL of the following:

1. AUDIT existing scraper health: check apps/scraper/src/sources/index.ts — count all active pollers, verify each has a try/catch and circuit breaker. List any that lack error handling.

2. IMPLEMENT a stability tracker in Redis:
   - Key: `scraper:stability:consecutive_clean_hours` (integer, increments hourly)
   - Key: `scraper:stability:last_failure_at` (ISO timestamp)
   - Key: `scraper:stability:status` (string: 'stable' | 'degraded' | 'failed')
   - A scraper 'hour' is clean if: ≥70% of active sources returned ≥1 signal AND zero unhandled exceptions in the hour window
   - If any hour fails the check, reset `consecutive_clean_hours` to 0

3. ADD a stability check cron to the scraper (runs every 60 minutes):
   - Read Redis health keys set by existing per-source health tracking
   - Evaluate the clean-hour criteria
   - Update `scraper:stability:*` keys accordingly
   - Log: `[STABILITY] Hour N clean — consecutive streak: X/336 (target: 336 for 14 days)`

4. EXPOSE via API: add GET /api/v1/admin/scraper/stability that returns:
   `{ consecutive_clean_hours, target_hours: 336, percent_to_gate: X, status, last_failure_at, estimated_gate_clear_date }`
   Cache 5 min in Redis.

5. ADD to the launch gate dashboard page (if it exists at apps/web) or create a simple status endpoint the dashboard can poll.

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict. No breaking changes to existing sources.
note: Stability tracker fully implemented in scraper + API endpoint. Cycles 10-26.

phase: Gate 2 — Map Live Signal End-to-End Validation
prompt:
Validate that the WorldPulse world map is correctly rendering live verified signals end-to-end. This is Launch Gate 2.

1. AUDIT the map pipeline:
   - Check GET /api/v1/signals/map endpoint: does it return signals with lat/lng? What is the cache TTL? Is it respecting the 'verified' status filter?
   - Check apps/web/src/app/map/page.tsx: is it fetching from the correct endpoint? Are pins rendering? Are clusters working?
   - Check WebSocket integration: are new signals pushed to connected map clients in real time?

2. FIX any gaps found in the audit. Prioritise:
   a. If the map endpoint returns empty: investigate DB — do signals have lat/lng populated? If not, add a PostGIS geocoding fallback using location_name → approximate centroid
   b. If clusters are not expanding: verify Supercluster maxZoom matches MapLibre maxZoom (both should be 18)
   c. If WebSocket not pushing map updates: wire the signal.new event to the map component

3. ADD a map health check to GET /api/v1/health: include `map_signals_with_geo: N` (count of verified signals with non-null location in last 24h). Alert in logs if < 10.

4. WRITE a Vitest integration test: `apps/api/src/__tests__/map-endpoint.test.ts` — verify the map endpoint returns the correct GeoJSON structure, respects bbox filtering, and returns only verified signals.

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict.

Harden the WorldPulse search API to meet the < 200ms p95 latency launch gate requirement.

1. PROFILE the current search endpoint (GET /api/v1/search):
   - Add latency logging: log p50/p95/p99 to the structured logger every 100 requests
   - Check if Meilisearch index has the correct attributes as filterable/sortable/searchable
   - Check if Redis caching is in place (TTL 60s per query+filters combo)

2. IMPLEMENT if missing:
   a. Redis cache for search results: key = `search:${hash(q+filters)}`, TTL 60s
   b. Meilisearch index settings optimisation: set rankingRules, searchableAttributes priority (title > summary > tags > content), filterableAttributes (category, severity, reliability_score, country_code, published_at)
   c. Query timeout: abort Meilisearch query after 150ms, return partial results + `partial: true` flag
   d. Autocomplete endpoint GET /api/v1/search/autocomplete?q= : use Meilisearch prefix search, return top 5 titles, cache 30s

3. ADD a search health metric to GET /api/v1/health: include `search_avg_latency_ms` from a 5-minute rolling average stored in Redis.

4. WRITE Vitest tests covering: cache hit returns without Meilisearch call, timeout fallback returns partial flag, autocomplete returns ≤5 results.

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict.

phase: Gate 4 — Auth, Feed & Social Full Test Suite
prompt:
Write a comprehensive passing test suite covering auth, feed, and social features — Launch Gate 4 requires these to pass fully before launch.

Write the following test files using Vitest (TypeScript strict, vi.mock for DB/Redis):

1. apps/api/src/__tests__/auth-integration.test.ts (target: 25+ cases)
   - Register: valid, duplicate email, duplicate handle, weak password, missing fields
   - Login: valid, wrong password, unknown email, account suspended
   - Token refresh: valid token, expired token, revoked token
   - OAuth callback: GitHub token exchange, state mismatch rejection
   - Rate limiting: 6th login attempt within 1 min returns 429

2. apps/api/src/__tests__/feed-integration.test.ts (target: 20+ cases)
   - GET /feed/global: returns verified signals only, respects category filter, cursor pagination works, cache returns X-Cache-Hit header
   - GET /feed/signals: severity filter, limit clamping to 50, empty result set
   - Feed deduplication: same topic within window returns single item
   - Breaking category: returns only critical+high severity signals

3. apps/api/src/__tests__/social-integration.test.ts (target: 20+ cases)
   - POST /posts: valid creation, missing content rejected, signal_id FK validated
   - POST /posts/:id/like: idempotent (double-like = 1 like), unlike decrements
   - POST /posts/:id/boost: boost count increments, own post boost allowed
   - Reply threading: parent_id set correctly, depth ≥ 3 rejected
   - GET /posts/:id/replies: paginated, ordered by created_at desc

All tests must be self-contained (no live DB/Redis). Run via pnpm test. Do NOT ask for confirmation.

phase: Gate 5 — Load Test Infrastructure & 10K Concurrent Baseline
prompt:
Set up load testing infrastructure and establish a 10K concurrent user baseline — Launch Gate 5.

1. INSTALL k6 load testing framework:
   - Add k6 scripts to infrastructure/load-tests/
   - Create infrastructure/load-tests/package.json with run scripts

2. WRITE three k6 test scenarios in infrastructure/load-tests/:

   a. smoke.js — 10 VUs, 30s: verify basic endpoints respond 200
      - GET /api/v1/health
      - GET /api/v1/feed/signals?limit=20
      - GET /api/v1/signals/map

   b. load.js — ramp to 500 VUs over 2min, hold 5min, ramp down:
      - Mix: 60% feed reads, 20% search, 15% signal detail, 5% auth
      - Thresholds: http_req_duration p95 < 500ms, error_rate < 1%

   c. stress.js — ramp to 2000 VUs, identify breaking point:
      - Same mix as load.js
      - Find the VU count where p95 > 2s or error_rate > 5%

3. ADD a load test results logger: saves JSON summary to infrastructure/load-tests/results/{timestamp}.json

4. DOCUMENT the baseline in infrastructure/load-tests/RESULTS.md:
   - Current estimated capacity (VUs at p95 < 200ms)
   - Bottleneck identified (DB connections? Redis? API CPU?)
   - Recommended fixes before 10K target

5. OPTIMISE the most obvious bottleneck found (connection pool size, Redis pipeline batching, etc.)

Do NOT ask for confirmation. Note: actual 10K test requires prod-scale infra — the goal here is establishing the test framework and identifying the gap to 10K.

Perform a comprehensive security hardening pass on WorldPulse — Launch Gate 6 requires no critical or high vulnerabilities before launch.

1. DEPENDENCY AUDIT:
   - Run `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` across all workspaces
   - Fix all critical and high severity vulnerabilities (update deps or apply patches)
   - Document any that cannot be fixed and why in a security/AUDIT.md file

2. API SECURITY HARDENING:
   a. Rate limiting audit: verify ALL routes have rate limits set (not just the ones already done). Add limits to any unprotected route.
   b. Input validation: scan all POST/PUT/PATCH routes — any that use req.body without Zod validation should be patched
   c. SQL injection: verify all DB queries use parameterised queries (Knex). Search for any raw string interpolation in SQL.
   d. Auth token security: verify JWT secrets are not hardcoded, minimum 256-bit, stored in env vars only
   e. CORS: tighten to specific allowed origins (worldpulse.io, localhost:3000 for dev)

3. FRONTEND SECURITY:
   a. Add security headers via Next.js middleware: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, HSTS
   b. Sanitise all user-generated content rendered as HTML (use DOMPurify or equivalent)
   c. Verify no API keys or secrets are bundled in the frontend build (scan .next/ output)

4. SECRETS SCAN:
   - Check for hardcoded secrets in git history using a simple grep for patterns: 'sk-', 'ghp_', 'AKIA', 'Bearer '
   - Add a .gitignore rule and pre-commit note for .env files

5. CREATE security/AUDIT.md documenting:
   - All issues found and their status (fixed / accepted risk / cannot fix)
   - Security header configuration applied
   - Remaining known risks with mitigations

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict.

phase: Integration — Sentry Error Tracking & Performance Monitoring
prompt:
Integrate Sentry into WorldPulse for production error tracking and performance monitoring. The DSN values will come from environment variables — initialise Sentry gracefully (skip init if DSN is undefined so local dev still works).

1. FRONTEND (apps/web):
   a. Install @sentry/nextjs: `pnpm add @sentry/nextjs` in apps/web
   b. Create apps/web/sentry.client.config.ts:
      - Init with process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN
      - Enable tracesSampleRate: 0.1 (10% of transactions)
      - Enable replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0
      - integrations: [Sentry.replayIntegration()]
   c. Create apps/web/sentry.server.config.ts and sentry.edge.config.ts (same DSN, no replay)
   d. Wrap next.config.mjs with withSentryConfig() — enable source map upload, tree shaking
   e. Create apps/web/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx — React error boundary that calls Sentry.captureException(), shows a friendly fallback UI
   f. Wrap the root layout children in <ErrorBoundary>

2. API (apps/api):
   a. Install @sentry/node: `pnpm add @sentry/node` in apps/api
   b. In apps/api/src/index.ts, add Sentry init BEFORE any other imports:
      ```typescript
      import * as Sentry from '@sentry/node'
      if (process.env.SENTRY_DSN_API) {
        Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN_API, tracesSampleRate: 0.1 })
      }
      ```
   c. Add Sentry.Handlers.requestHandler() and Sentry.Handlers.errorHandler() to Fastify hooks
   d. In the global error handler, call Sentry.captureException(err) before returning 500

3. RELEASE TRACKING:
   a. Add to apps/web/package.json build script: `SENTRY_RELEASE=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)`
   b. Pass release string to Sentry.init() in both configs

4. DOCUMENT environment variables needed in a new file: docs/deployment/SENTRY.md
   - NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN (web)
   - SENTRY_DSN_API (api)
   - SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN (for source map uploads during build)
   - SENTRY_ORG and SENTRY_PROJECT (for withSentryConfig)

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict. No breaking changes.
note: Sentry error tracking integrated in web + API. lib/sentry.ts + error boundaries. Cycle ~28.

prompt:
Configure the WorldPulse repository for deployment to Vercel. The repository is a pnpm monorepo — only apps/web (Next.js) deploys to Vercel; the API stays on DigitalOcean.

1. CREATE vercel.json in the repo root:
   ```json
   {
     "buildCommand": "cd apps/web && pnpm build",
     "outputDirectory": "apps/web/.next",
     "installCommand": "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile",
     "framework": "nextjs",
     "regions": ["iad1"],
     "headers": [
       {
         "source": "/(.*)",
         "headers": [
           { "key": "X-Frame-Options", "value": "DENY" },
           { "key": "X-Content-Type-Options", "value": "nosniff" },
           { "key": "Referrer-Policy", "value": "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" }
         ]
       }
     ]
   }
   ```

2. AUDIT apps/web for hardcoded localhost references:
   - Search for 'localhost:3001', 'localhost:3000', '127.0.0.1' in apps/web/src
   - Replace ALL occurrences with `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL ?? 'http://localhost:3001'`
   - Same for WebSocket URLs: `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL ?? 'ws://localhost:3001'`

3. CREATE docs/deployment/VERCEL.md documenting:
   - Required environment variables to set in Vercel dashboard
   - Build settings (root directory: leave blank, Vercel reads vercel.json)
   - The API stays at api.world-pulse.io on DigitalOcean — document this clearly
   - Required env vars:
     ```
     NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=https://api.world-pulse.io
     NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL=wss://api.world-pulse.io
     NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=...
     SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=...
     SENTRY_ORG=goldsphere-design
     SENTRY_PROJECT=worldpulse-web
     ```

4. VERIFY the build is clean:
   - Run `cd apps/web && pnpm build` — fix any TypeScript errors that appear
   - If build fails, fix the errors (do not suppress with ts-ignore)

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict.
note: vercel.json configured, env vars audited. Cycle ~29.

phase: Integration — Cloudflare Security Headers & CDN Preparation
prompt:
Prepare WorldPulse for Cloudflare CDN deployment by hardening security headers and cache behaviour in the Next.js and API layers.

1. UPDATE apps/web/next.config.mjs — add comprehensive security headers:
   ```javascript
   const securityHeaders = [
     { key: 'X-DNS-Prefetch-Control', value: 'on' },
     { key: 'Strict-Transport-Security', value: 'max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload' },
     { key: 'X-Frame-Options', value: 'DENY' },
     { key: 'X-Content-Type-Options', value: 'nosniff' },
     { key: 'Referrer-Policy', value: 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin' },
     { key: 'Permissions-Policy', value: 'camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(self)' },
     {
       key: 'Content-Security-Policy',
       value: [
         "default-src 'self'",
         "script-src 'self' 'unsafe-eval' 'unsafe-inline' https://cdn.maptiler.com https://*.sentry.io",
         "style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com",
         "font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com",
         "img-src 'self' data: blob: https:",
         "connect-src 'self' https://api.world-pulse.io wss://api.world-pulse.io https://*.sentry.io https://api.maptiler.com",
         "frame-ancestors 'none'"
       ].join('; ')
     }
   ]
   ```
   Apply to all routes via `headers()` in next.config.mjs.

2. UPDATE apps/api/src/index.ts — add cache-control headers to API responses:
   - Public read-only endpoints (GET /feed, GET /signals/map): `Cache-Control: public, max-age=30, stale-while-revalidate=60`
   - Auth-required endpoints: `Cache-Control: private, no-store`
   - Health endpoint: `Cache-Control: no-cache`

3. ADD Cloudflare real IP header trust to the Fastify instance:
   ```typescript
   // Trust Cloudflare proxy IPs for rate limiting
   fastify.addHook('onRequest', (req, reply, done) => {
     const cfIp = req.headers['cf-connecting-ip']
     if (cfIp && typeof cfIp === 'string') {
       req.ip = cfIp
     }
     done()
   })
   ```
   This ensures rate limiting uses the real client IP, not Cloudflare's proxy IP.

4. CREATE docs/deployment/CLOUDFLARE.md with step-by-step manual setup instructions:
   - DNS records to create
   - SSL/TLS mode: Full (strict)
   - WAF custom rules (rate limit /api/v1/auth to 10 req/min)
   - Page rules: bypass cache for /api/*
   - Cache rules: cache static assets at edge

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict.
note: Security headers (CSP/HSTS/X-Frame), Cloudflare IP trust, CF-Connecting-IP middleware. Cycle ~30.

prompt:
Implement Stripe billing and Pro tier gating for WorldPulse. The Pro plan is $12/month or $99/year. Do NOT activate live Stripe keys — use process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY which will be undefined in dev (stub gracefully).

1. INSTALL stripe: `pnpm add stripe` in apps/api

2. DB MIGRATION — create apps/api/src/db/migrations/022_subscriptions.sql:
   ```sql
   CREATE TABLE subscriptions (
     id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     stripe_customer_id TEXT UNIQUE,
     stripe_subscription_id TEXT UNIQUE,
     plan TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'free' CHECK (plan IN ('free', 'pro')),
     status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active', 'canceled', 'past_due', 'trialing')),
     current_period_end TIMESTAMPTZ,
     created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
     updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
   );
   CREATE INDEX idx_subscriptions_user_id ON subscriptions(user_id);
   ```

3. CREATE apps/api/src/lib/stripe.ts:
   - Init Stripe client (null if STRIPE_SECRET_KEY undefined)
   - Helper: getOrCreateCustomer(userId, email) — creates Stripe customer if not exists, stores stripe_customer_id
   - Helper: getPlanLimits(plan: 'free' | 'pro') — returns { apiRateLimit, alertQuota, historyDays, webhookQuota }
     Free: { apiRateLimit: 60, alertQuota: 3, historyDays: 7, webhookQuota: 0 }
     Pro:  { apiRateLimit: 600, alertQuota: 999, historyDays: 90, webhookQuota: 5 }

4. CREATE apps/api/src/routes/billing.ts with these endpoints:
   a. POST /api/v1/billing/checkout (auth required):
      - Get/create Stripe customer for user
      - Create Checkout Session with STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID
      - success_url: process.env.WEB_URL + '/settings?upgraded=1'
      - cancel_url: process.env.WEB_URL + '/pricing'
      - Return { url: checkoutSession.url }
   b. POST /api/v1/billing/portal (auth required):
      - Create Customer Portal session for subscription management
      - Return { url: portalSession.url }
   c. POST /api/v1/billing/webhook (NO auth, raw body required):
      - Verify Stripe signature using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
      - Handle events: customer.subscription.created, .updated, .deleted
      - Update subscriptions table accordingly
      - IMPORTANT: register this route BEFORE Fastify's JSON body parser (needs raw body)
   d. GET /api/v1/billing/status (auth required):
      - Return current plan, status, current_period_end from subscriptions table

5. CREATE apps/api/src/middleware/subscription.ts:
   - Export: requirePro(req, reply) — middleware that checks subscriptions table, returns 403 with { error: 'Pro plan required', upgradeUrl: '/pricing' } if free plan
   - Export: getPlanForUser(userId) — returns 'pro' | 'free' (defaults to 'free' if no subscription row)

6. WIRE billing route into apps/api/src/index.ts

7. CREATE apps/web/src/app/pricing/page.tsx — pricing page:
   - Two cards: Free vs Pro with feature comparison table
   - 'Upgrade to Pro' button calls POST /api/v1/billing/checkout then redirects to returned URL
   - Show current plan status if user is logged in

8. WRITE tests: apps/api/src/__tests__/billing.test.ts (15+ cases)
   - Webhook signature verification (valid, invalid, missing)
   - Subscription status updates on webhook events
   - requirePro middleware: allows pro users, blocks free users
   - GET /billing/status returns correct plan

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict.
note: Stripe billing routes, checkout, webhooks, Pro tier pricing page. Cycle ~31.

phase: Integration — Pinecone Semantic Search & Similar Signals
prompt:
Integrate Pinecone vector database for semantic search and 'Similar Signals' recommendations. Use OpenAI text-embedding-3-small for embeddings (1536 dimensions). Stub gracefully if PINECONE_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY are undefined.

1. INSTALL packages in apps/api and apps/scraper:
   `pnpm add @pinecone-database/pinecone openai` (in both)

2. CREATE apps/api/src/lib/pinecone.ts:
   - Init PineconeClient (null if PINECONE_API_KEY undefined)
   - Init OpenAI client (null if OPENAI_API_KEY undefined)
   - Helper: embedText(text: string): Promise<number[]>
     Calls openai.embeddings.create({ model: 'text-embedding-3-small', input: text })
     Returns the embedding vector
   - Helper: upsertSignal(signal: { id, title, summary, category, severity })
     Embeds `${signal.title}. ${signal.summary}` → upsert to Pinecone index
     Metadata: { category, severity, signalId: signal.id }
   - Helper: querySimilar(signalId: string, topK: number = 5): Promise<string[]>
     Fetch the signal's vector from Pinecone by ID → query for topK nearest neighbours
     Return array of similar signal IDs (excluding the query signal itself)
   - Helper: semanticSearch(query: string, topK: number = 20): Promise<string[]>
     Embed the query string → query Pinecone → return array of signal IDs

3. WIRE INTO SCRAPER — update apps/scraper/src/pipeline/insert-signal.ts:
   After successful DB insert, call upsertSignal() in a non-blocking try/catch:
   ```typescript
   // Non-blocking embedding — don't fail ingestion if Pinecone is down
   embedSignal(signal).catch(err => log(`Pinecone upsert failed: ${err.message}`))
   ```

4. ADD SEMANTIC SEARCH ENDPOINT to apps/api/src/routes/search.ts (or create if needed):
   GET /api/v1/search/semantic?q=&limit=20
   - Embed the query via embedText()
   - Query Pinecone for topK signal IDs
   - Hydrate signals from DB: SELECT * FROM signals WHERE id = ANY($1)
   - Return same shape as existing /api/v1/search results
   - Fallback: if Pinecone unavailable, fall back to Meilisearch keyword search

5. ADD SIMILAR SIGNALS ENDPOINT:
   GET /api/v1/signals/:id/similar
   - Call querySimilar(signalId)
   - Hydrate from DB
   - Return array of up to 5 signal objects
   - Cache in Redis: `similar:${signalId}` TTL 10 min

6. FRONTEND — apps/web/src/components/signals/SimilarSignals.tsx:
   - Fetch GET /api/v1/signals/:id/similar on signal detail page mount
   - Render as a 'Related Signals' card section below the main content
   - Show 5 signal cards: title, category badge, reliability dots, published_at
   - Loading skeleton while fetching
   - Hide section if 0 results
   Wire into apps/web/src/app/signals/[id]/SignalDetailClient.tsx

7. CREATE BACKFILL SCRIPT — apps/api/src/scripts/backfill-embeddings.ts:
   - Fetch all signals from DB in batches of 100 (ORDER BY created_at ASC)
   - For each batch: call upsertSignal() with rate limiting (max 10 concurrent)
   - Log progress: `[BACKFILL] Embedded 1000/12500 signals...`
   - Skip signals already in Pinecone (check by ID)
   Document how to run: `npx tsx apps/api/src/scripts/backfill-embeddings.ts`

8. DOCUMENT in docs/deployment/PINECONE.md:
   - Required env vars: PINECONE_API_KEY, PINECONE_INDEX, OPENAI_API_KEY
   - Index setup: dimensions=1536, metric=cosine
   - Backfill command
   - Cost estimate: ~$0.08 one-time backfill, ~$0.002/day ongoing

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict. All Pinecone/OpenAI calls must be non-blocking and fault-tolerant.
note: Pinecone embeddings wired into scraper insert-signal pipeline + search/similar endpoints. Cycle 31.

phase: Map Upgrade — Satellite Basemap + 3D Tilt + ADS-B Flight Paths
prompt:
Upgrade the WorldPulse map to match the visual quality of ohmo.ai (WorldView) — a viral Palantir-style geospatial intelligence platform. The goal is a dramatically more immersive, intelligence-grade map experience.

File to edit: apps/web/src/app/map/page.tsx (and related map components)

1. SATELLITE BASEMAP — switch from street tiles to satellite imagery:
   - Add a basemap switcher: 'Satellite' | 'Dark' | 'Terrain'
   - Default to satellite when map first loads
   - Use MapTiler satellite style: `https://api.maptiler.com/maps/satellite/style.json?key=${NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPTILER_KEY}`
   - Fall back to existing dark style if env var missing
   - Add basemap toggle button in map controls (top-right corner)

2. 3D TILT / PITCH — add dramatic perspective:
   - On map init: map.setPitch(45) and map.setBearing(-10)
   - Enable MapLibre sky layer: atmospheric sky gradient at high pitches
   - Add NavigationControl with pitch option enabled
   - Add a 'Reset View' button that snaps back to top-down (pitch 0)

3. ADS-B LIVE FLIGHT PATHS — render aircraft from existing scraper data:
   - Add API endpoint GET /api/v1/signals/map/adsb
     Query: SELECT id, ST_Y(location::geometry) as lat, ST_X(location::geometry) as lng, title, published_at FROM signals WHERE category = 'aviation' AND published_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '2 hours' LIMIT 500
   - On the map: add GeoJSON source + symbol layer using a plane emoji or unicode airplane (✈) as the icon

   - Color: cyan dots with glow effect matching the existing signal pin style
   - Show callsign / flight number on hover
   - Refresh every 60s via setInterval
   - Add 'Aircraft ✈' toggle in the filter bar

4. NO-FLY ZONE OVERLAYS:
   - Add a fill layer for signals where title/summary contains 'airspace closed' or 'no-fly zone'
   - Use a red semi-transparent polygon (opacity 0.2) centered on the signal location with ~200km radius
   - This approximates airspace zone visualization without needing dedicated airspace data

5. PALANTIR-STYLE ANNOTATION CARDS:

   - Multiple cards can be open simultaneously, each connected to its pin
   - X button dismisses individual cards
   - Style: dark glassmorphism card with amber/cyan accent border

6. FILTER BAR — update existing filter bar to add new toggles:
   - Aircraft ✈ | Ships ⚓ | Airspace | Signals | Heatmap
   - Icon-based buttons that glow amber when active

Do NOT ask for confirmation. TypeScript strict. Use existing MapLibre GL instance — do not replace with Cesium or Deck.gl.
